page: Microsoft Azure D8s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The instance features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, which establishes a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. The processor includes a substantial 480 MB L3 cache, along with 2 MB L2 and 48 KB L1d caches, and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for vector and matrix acceleration.

This virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The D8s_v7 does not include any local storage, though it is designated as Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses, nor does it feature any dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the D8s_v7 demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, particularly in compression tasks. It achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression at 36.55 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 15.22 GB/sec. General compute benchmarks place the instance in the average tier, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2276.39 and a multi-core score of 9308.24. Memory performance is balanced, yielding a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 689.31 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 40.61 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning workloads, the instance achieves a strong text generation speed of 271.19 tokens/sec using a small 135M parameter model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D8s_v7 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of local storage. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and strong single-core compression capabilities, this instance is well-suited for web serving, database operations, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores and 32.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks show top-tier capabilities in single-threaded compression, with gzip compression reaching 36.55 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 15.22 GB/sec. Other metrics, such as a stress-ng multi-core score of 9308.24 and a Redis SET throughput of 16.48 million operations per second, fall within the average tier. This server is optimized for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v7 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier single-threaded compression performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v7 is an 8-vCPU, 32 GB RAM general-purpose virtual machine powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor with no local storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed cache, and top-tier single-threaded compression performance.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz with 8 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, offering a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
Top-tier single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression.
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPUs.
Suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database operations.
